post_topic: Azure-to-AWS mental model translation as an onboarding accelerator
image_description:
Single standalone diagram. One concept only. No panels, no grids, no collages. Full bleed 1080x1080px white background.

A two-column vertical comparison table titled 'Cloud Equivalents: AWS → Azure' in bold navy text at the top center.

Left column header: 'AWS' with the AWS smile-arrow logo next to it. Right column header: 'Azure' with the Azure logo next to it. A thin vertical dividing line runs between the two columns.

Five rows of service pairs, evenly spaced top to bottom:
Row 1: 'S3' (left) ↔ 'Blob Storage' (right)
Row 2: 'IAM Roles' (left) ↔ 'Managed Identities' (right)
Row 3: 'ECS Fargate' (left) ↔ 'Container Apps' (right)
Row 4: 'Route 53' (left) ↔ 'Azure DNS' (right)
Row 5: 'CloudWatch' (left) ↔ 'Azure Monitor' (right)

Each row has a horizontal double-headed arrow (↔) in blue #5a96ff connecting the two service names.

Below three of the rows — IAM Roles, ECS Fargate, and CloudWatch — add a small amber #F5A623 callout tag reading 'Note: auth model differs', 'Note: scaling config differs', and 'Note: alerting structure differs' respectively, positioned to the far right of each row.

At the bottom of the diagram, a single line in smaller navy text: 'Same mental shape. Different surface. Know where the map breaks.'

Navy #0A0F1E primary elements, blue #5a96ff highlights, amber #F5A623 callouts and important labels. Clean sans-serif font. Real cloud provider logos where relevant. Small text 'Kelvin Davis' bottom right corner. Professional LinkedIn infographic style similar to ByteByteGo. White background. No dark backgrounds.
